behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (summary writer must query stored dimension findings by round). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process DB/repo is enough. LLM-DEP: none; verdicts are already stored. TWO-PASS: down-level to Unit was rejected because single-function logic would miss repository/query scoping bugs across summary and findings stores. Up-level to System was rejected because behavior text only requires checking stored findings for the same round, not real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Wire summary-save service to a real local persistence layer (or repo implementation). Seed same-round findings including at least one FAIL, attempt to persist PASS summary, assert rejection and no PASS state transition is stored.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears when gate/orchestrator consumes summary text instead of findings repository data. REAL-INFRA: no external OS/network dependency is required. LLM-DEP: none; this is deterministic gate logic over stored records. TWO-PASS: Unit rejected because it can’t prove real wiring path reads repository data. Agentic rejected because behavior text is explicitly about data source selection, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run gate computation with conflicting inputs: summary text says PASS while stored findings include FAIL. Assert gate verdict is non-PASS based on stored findings and that changing summary text alone does not flip verdict.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure spans request parsing/policy enforcement/audit recording boundaries. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with local modules and local persistence; no real OS/network is required. LLM-DEP: none. TWO-PASS: Unit rejected because it would miss failures where override is accepted but warning record is not persisted. System rejected because behavior text asks for auditable record, not external log transport guarantees.
behaviors.test_description: Submit manual summary update without override flag and assert rejection. Submit with override flag and assert acceptance plus persisted warning/audit record containing actor, round, and reason metadata.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is cross-module consistency between fix-commit metadata and review-round metadata used by gate. REAL-INFRA: real git/OS behavior is not strictly required if commit and round lineage can be represented in local test fixtures. LLM-DEP: identifier freshness check is deterministic and does not depend on model judgment. TWO-PASS: Unit rejected because ordering/lineage bugs emerge when gate, commit tracker, and review-round store interact. Agentic rejected because behavior text requires temporal relation ('produced after latest fix commit'), not output quality from model.
behaviors.test_description: Create fixtures with latest fix commit C2 and existing round tied to earlier commit C1. Assert PASS finalization is blocked. Add new round identifier tied to post-fix commit C2 and assert gate can proceed (subject to other checks).

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior explicitly requires a real review-agent round in the live loop; execution path includes real model call, and stubs can hide failures where run bookkeeping occurs without actual model execution. MOCK-HIDE: mocked agent responses can falsely satisfy 'fresh run' without proving real invocation on updated diff. MULTI-STEP: mainly one required agentic decision/run boundary, so Agentic over Workflow. TWO-PASS: Integration rejected because behavior text requires 'live review loop' and 'review-agent round runs' on updated diff, disqualifying pure deterministic stubs.
behaviors.test_description: On updated diff after fix commit, attempt PASS finalization before running agent round and assert block. Execute an actual review-agent run against the updated diff, persist returned round id/metadata, then assert PASS can proceed only after that fresh run exists.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires state-machine + findings-store interaction (round counter, unresolved MUST-FIX check, outcome mapping). REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: decision is based on stored results, not evaluating model output quality at assertion time. TWO-PASS: Unit rejected because it can miss integration bugs where exhaustion counter and findings query diverge. Workflow rejected because behavior text anchors on stored unresolved findings and exhausted count, not validating multi-agent content quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed stored results with unresolved MUST-FIX items and mark max rounds reached. Run gate outcome computation and assert escalation status is emitted and PASS is not possible.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP: behavior explicitly targets a live multi-round workflow, requiring sequential round progression plus terminal gate decision. LLM-DEP: each round is agent-driven; handoff correctness depends on sequenced agentic stages and final gate evaluation. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: not Integration because (c) multiple agentic steps are explicitly chained ('live multi-round review workflow'). TWO-PASS: Agentic-only rejected because single-step agent test cannot expose failures in cross-round exhaustion/handoff logic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end multi-round review run on live agent loop until round budget is exhausted while unresolved MUST-FIX findings remain in stored outputs. Assert PASS remains blocked and escalation event/record is emitted at workflow termination.
